div.span:
- Currently the connected nodes are the same node in the app graph
- If they aren't the same node, how do we we consisently id graph nodes?
- Node ids need to be unique
- We also need to figure out how we will deterministically reveal nodes in
- Similarly we need a better url-mapping, both in terms of precision of
- How/where to expose feed objects in the app graph?

span.div: - increases the complexity oft the layout state

div.span: - graph builders automatically create unique ids for nodes based on the path
to that node from the root, concatenating the id returned for the node in the
graph builder with the path to that point
- This would automatically differentiate all nodes and provide them with very

div.font: unique ids based on the path
- if we disallow / in graph builder ids, then graph builder could use / as the path
separator when generating ids for nodes
- using / as the separator would allow us to map the node id directly to the url

div.div: - it doesn't have an answer to how to make urls friendlier
- if the id of an object is something like <space-id>/documents/all/<document-id>
we then need to figure out how to navigate to it if all we have is the document
- increases the complexity of graph builder
On Feed Objects in the App Graph
- there's an experiment that we used with the space settings panels where when

one
of those is attended but the "alternate tree" isn't visible, we show the attention

signifier
at its ancestor, the space node, which happens to be in the L1 header
- I wonder if we could adopt a similar pattern for feed objects
- For example, My Mail might have children for its messages that are marked as
hidden so that they don't all pollute the nav tree
- These nodes could be added lazily such that by default there are none and they
are only added once one is directly navigated to
- For orienting the user, we could consider expanding the pattern of showing the
attention signifier for the ancestor to showing current as the ancestor as well
- This would allow us to mark My Mail in the navtree as the current item when its
hidden child, a feed message object, is open
- proposals would make these even longer /<space-id>/types/<type-dxn>/<object-dxn>
- one thing that would help is partial id lookup, so rather than space id, it would
- identifiers for things like types being urls already causes challenges for url mapping
- something that would help address that is taking the approach that atproto did
for lexicon namespaces which is to use reverse dns names (e.g. app.bsky.feed.post
- applying all of this we'd end up with a url for a document that looks something like
- for object ids, I think we might need to use the end of the ids rather than the beginning
since that is the part that is random and less likely to have collisions within a space
- if this path seems reasonable we can set it aside for now as its purely additive to other
On Knowing the Whole Path to Navigate
- similar to how routers provide path helpers, I think that app-toolkit and plugins can
provide helpers which make it easier to computer the id/path of an object for one to
- for example, app-toolkit can provide the canonical location where all non-system
objects will be visible: getTypesPath(space, object)
